steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The blank fills "As a result, _____ will begin to combust." The antecedent is "the substance" (coconut oil), introduced two sentences earlier as a singular noun.

steps.title: Rule: a pronoun must match its antecedent in number and function
steps.content:
Per-choice diagnosis:

A. "they" — plural pronoun; "the substance" is singular. Agreement fails. Incorrect.

B. "it" — singular pronoun matching singular "the substance." Substitution: "the substance will begin to combust" — meaning holds. Correct.

C. "those" — plural demonstrative; does not agree with singular "the substance." Incorrect.

D. "that" — singular demonstrative, but "that" is used to point to something more distant or to introduce a clause, not as the standard subject pronoun for a previously named substance. "It" is the conventional choice. Incorrect.

B is correct.
